resource.note: tight — multiple 'now' tier patients in queue

warnings: Three 'now' tier patients ahead in queue — this case must not displace them
Tetanus booster status: last 4y ago, within 5y window, no booster needed for clean wound

monitor.action: Wound care: irrigate, explore for tendon/nerve involvement, close with sutures or skin adhesive
monitor.reason: Single-resource visit (simple suture) consistent with ESI 4.

monitor.action: Reassess neurovascular status of L index finger (cap refill, 2-point discrimination, flexion/extension)
monitor.reason: Rule out occult tendon or digital nerve injury before closure.

explanation.summary: 34F with isolated 2cm clean finger laceration, bleeding controlled, normal vitals, no comorbidities or anticoagulants. Single-resource visit (suture). Safely waits behind higher-acuity patients; could also be redirected to fast-track if available.

checklist.task: Physician closure (sutures/adhesive) and tendon/nerve exam documentation
Confirm tetanus status — last booster 4y ago, no update needed for clean wound
Discharge instructions: wound care, return precautions, suture removal timing

why_this_tier: Single resource expected (suture only) — ESI 4 pattern
No red flags: no anticoagulants, no comorbidities, bleeding controlled, low pain
Three 'now' tier patients in queue must take priority
would_upgrade_if: Exam reveals tendon laceration, digital nerve deficit, or arterial bleeding
Wound found to be deeper/contaminated requiring imaging or OR consult
New vital sign abnormality on reassessment
would_downgrade_if: Wound suitable for skin adhesive only (no suture) — ESI 5 territory
